1.english: The Inevitable Reality -
What is the Inevitable Reality?
And what can make you know what is the Inevitable Reality?
Thamud and 'Aad denied the Striking Calamity.
So as for Thamud, they were destroyed by the overpowering [blast].

1.arabic: وأما عاد فأهلكوا بريح صرصر عاتية
1.english: And as for 'Aad, they were destroyed by a screaming, violent wind
1.transliteration: Wa ammaa 'Aadun fa uhlikoo bireehin sarsarin 'aatiyah

1.arabic: سخرها عليهم سبع ليال وثمانية أيام حسوما فترى القوم فيها صرعى كأنهم أعجاز نخل خاوية
1.english: Which Allah imposed upon them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so you would see the people therein fallen as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees.
1.transliteration: Sakhkharahaa 'alaihim sab'a la yaalinw wa samaaniyata ayyaamin husooman fataral qawma feehaa sar'aa ka annahum a'jaazu nakhlin khaawiyah

1.english: Then do you see of them any remains?

1.arabic: وجاء فرعون ومن قبله والمؤتفكات بالخاطئة
1.english: And there came Pharaoh and those before him and the overturned cities with sin.
1.transliteration: Wa jaaa'a Firawnu wa man qablahoo wal mu'tafikaatu bilhaati'ah

1.arabic: فعصوا رسول ربهم فأخذهم أخذة رابية
1.english: And they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a seizure exceeding [in severity].
1.transliteration: Fa'ansaw Rasoola Rabbihim fa akhazahum akhzatar raabiyah

1.arabic: إنا لما طغى الماء حملناكم في الجارية
1.english: Indeed, when the water overflowed, We carried your ancestors in the sailing ship
1.transliteration: Innaa lammaa taghal maaa'u hamalnaakum fil jaariyah

1.arabic: لنجعلها لكم تذكرة وتعيها أذن واعية
1.english: That We might make it for you a reminder and [that] a conscious ear would be conscious of it.
1.transliteration: Linaj'alahaa lakum tazki ratanw-wa ta'iyahaa unzununw waa'iyah

1.arabic: فإذا نفخ في الصور نفخة واحدة
1.english: Then when the Horn is blown with one blast
1.transliteration: Fa izaa nufikha fis soori nafkhatunw waahidah

1.arabic: وحملت الأرض والجبال فدكتا دكة واحدة
1.english: And the earth and the mountains are lifted and leveled with one blow -
1.transliteration: Wa humilatil ardu wal jibaalu fadukkataa dakkatanw waahidah

1.english: Then on that Day, the Resurrection will occur,
And the heaven will split [open], for that Day it is infirm.

1.arabic: والملك على أرجائها ۚ ويحمل عرش ربك فوقهم يومئذ ثمانية
1.english: And the angels are at its edges. And there will bear the Throne of your Lord above them, that Day, eight [of them].
1.transliteration: Wal malaku 'alaaa arjaaa'ihaa; wa yahmilu 'Arsha Rabbika fawqahum yawma'izin samaaniyah

1.arabic: يومئذ تعرضون لا تخفى منكم خافية
1.english: That Day, you will be exhibited [for judgement]; not hidden among you is anything concealed.
1.transliteration: Yawma'izin tu'radoona laa takhfaa min kum khaafiyah

1.arabic: فأما من أوتي كتابه بيمينه فيقول هاؤم اقرءوا كتابيه
1.english: So as for he who is given his record in his right hand, he will say, "Here, read my record!
1.transliteration: Fa ammaa man ootiya kitaabahoo biyameenihee fa yaqoolu haaa'umuq ra'oo kitaabiyah

1.english: Indeed, I was certain that I would be meeting my account."
So he will be in a pleasant life -
In an elevated garden,
Its [fruit] to be picked hanging near.

1.arabic: كلوا واشربوا هنيئا بما أسلفتم في الأيام الخالية
1.english: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you put forth in the days past."
1.transliteration: Kuloo washraboo haneee'am bimaaa aslaftum fil ayyaamil khaliyah

1.arabic: وأما من أوتي كتابه بشماله فيقول يا ليتني لم أوت كتابيه
1.english: But as for he who is given his record in his left hand, he will say, "Oh, I wish I had not been given my record
1.transliteration: Wa ammaa man ootiya kitaabahoo bishimaalihee fa yaqoolu yaalaitanee lam oota kitaaabiyah

1.english: And had not known what is my account.
I wish my death had been the decisive one.
My wealth has not availed me.
Gone from me is my authority."
[Allah will say], "Seize him and shackle him.
Then into Hellfire drive him.

1.arabic: ثم في سلسلة ذرعها سبعون ذراعا فاسلكوه
1.english: Then into a chain whose length is seventy cubits insert him."
1.transliteration: Summa fee silsilatin zar'uhaa sab'oona ziraa'an faslukooh

1.arabic: إنه كان لا يؤمن بالله العظيم
1.english: Indeed, he did not used to believe in Allah, the Most Great,
Nor did he encourage the feeding of the poor.
So there is not for him here this Day any devoted friend
Nor any food except from the discharge of wounds;
1.transliteration: Wa laa ta'aamun illaa min ghisleen

1.english: None will eat it except the sinners.
So I swear by what you see
And what you do not see
[That] indeed, the Qur'an is the word of a noble Messenger.

1.arabic: وما هو بقول شاعر ۚ قليلا ما تؤمنون
1.english: And it is not the word of a poet; little do you believe.
1.transliteration: Wa ma huwa biqawli shaa'ir; qaleelam maa tu'minoon

1.arabic: ولا بقول كاهن ۚ قليلا ما تذكرون
1.english: Nor the word of a soothsayer; little do you remember.
1.transliteration: Wa laa biqawli kaahin; qaleelam maa tazakkaroon

1.english: [It is] a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.
And if Muhammad had made up about Us some [false] sayings,
We would have seized him by the right hand;
Then We would have cut from him the aorta.

1.arabic: فما منكم من أحد عنه حاجزين
1.english: And there is no one of you who could prevent [Us] from him.
And indeed, the Qur'an is a reminder for the righteous.
And indeed, We know that among you are deniers.
1.transliteration: Wa inna lana'lamu anna minkum mukazzibeen

1.english: And indeed, it will be [a cause of] regret upon the disbelievers.
And indeed, it is the truth of certainty.
So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.
